quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an LLM tournament judging candidate quality and producing a ranking that selects candidates for experimental evaluation.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reports agreement with human experts and intra-rater consistency for this exact judge, i.e. an agreement study, not calibration.

quotes.quote: Given a disease, Robin automatically identifies relevant in vitro assays that model key disease mechanisms and proposes specific drug candidates to evaluate in these experimental models.
quotes.rationale: Establishes that ranked candidates advance to the experimental stage.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Empirical assay results are analysed and routed back into generating revised candidates.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Analysis of experimental data is split between human and automated components, making the substrate hybrid.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: Human analysis of part of the physical measurement evidence in the same loop.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Human researchers gate whether proposed experiments are actually run.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: States the system's outputs are not directly executable and require human translation, a human-held executability check.

diagnostics.consequence: Agreement with expert preference cannot license claims that the ranking selects candidates that succeed in assay; the ranking channel's predictive validity remains unestablished.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

diagnostics.statement: The reported reliability evidence for the LLM tournament judge is an expert-agreement and intra-rater consistency study; no calibration, bias test, or outcome-anchored validation against experimental results is reported for the ranking channel.

diagnostics.consequence: The empirical validation loop is not closed computationally; human interpretation of underspecified outlines is an uncontrolled variable in every reported result.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

diagnostics.statement: The system's outputs are experimental outlines rather than executable protocols, so human translation is required before any candidate is empirically tested.

diagnostics.consequence: The claim of autonomous analysis driving the next candidate round cannot be attributed cleanly to the system, weakening any autonomy claim about the empirical feedback path.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.statement: Data analysis in the experimental feedback loop is split between human scientists and automated components, and the packet does not report which analytic decisions were human versus automated beyond two named steps.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report an outcome-anchored validation of the LLM tournament ranking, comparing candidate rank against measured assay results rather than only against expert preference and self-consistency.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Rank-outcome association exceeding a random-ordering baseline at a pre-stated significance level
success_check.evidence_needed: Physical assay measurements for a rank-stratified sample of candidates, with the ranking fixed before testing
success_check.observable: Association between tournament rank and measured assay effect size across all ranked candidates, including low-ranked ones

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

recommendations.proposed_action: Publish the protocol translation step, recording what human experimenters added to each generated outline before execution.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Complete pairing available for every experiment contributing to reported results
success_check.evidence_needed: Released outline-to-protocol diffs or an equivalent execution log
success_check.observable: Paired record of each generated outline and the executed protocol, with human-added specifications marked

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

recommendations.proposed_action: State, per analysis step in the experimental loop, whether the step was performed by a human or by the automated component, and which steps fed the next candidate round.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every analysis step labelled human, automated, or joint, with no unattributed steps
success_check.evidence_needed: Methods-level provenance listing covering all data modalities used to generate subsequent candidate rounds
success_check.observable: Step-level attribution table for the analysis pipeline of each reported experimental round

policy_type: LLM-judged tournament ranking over generated drug candidates
iterative round-based candidate regeneration conditioned on experimental results
